skill.name: webflow-automation
skill.description: Guide Webflow CMS, publishing, pages, assets, and ecommerce workflows through Rube MCP and Composio Webflow tools.
skill.summary: Automate common Webflow site and CMS operations through Rube MCP with schema-first tool discovery.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and cryptography indicators, but review found they are Markdown inline tool names, parameters, ID examples, and an MD5 checksum requirement. The real risk is operational: this community skill drives OAuth-backed Webflow actions through Rube MCP, including publishing and permanent CMS deletion, so it should be published only with clear user confirmation warnings.

medium_findings.title: OAuth-Backed Webflow Network Automation
medium_findings.description: The skill connects to Rube MCP and Webflow OAuth, then uses scoped Webflow tools. This is expected behavior, but it can access site, CMS, page, asset, and order data through external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly requires Rube MCP, Webflow OAuth connection management, and Webflow OAuth scopes. This confirms networked account access, but it is aligned with the skill purpose.

medium_findings.title: Production-Affecting Webflow Actions
medium_findings.description: The skill includes site publishing and permanent CMS item deletion workflows. It warns users to confirm these actions, but mistakes could affect live Webflow sites.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines state that publishing deploys all staged changes and deletion is permanent. The same lines also include confirmation guidance, reducing malicious concern.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown Tool References
low_findings.description: The repeated external command findings are inline Markdown references to Rube and Webflow tool names or parameters. No shell command, Ruby backtick execution, or local process execution is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation and the flagged backticks surround tool slugs and parameter names. I found no executable Ruby or shell code in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography lines are front matter and a section heading. The only hash-related guidance is MD5 for Webflow asset upload checksums, not password storage or security-sensitive hashing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Lines 3 and 212 do not contain cryptographic code. The MD5 reference appears only in asset upload metadata, which supports a checksum use.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Remote Webflow Discovery
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings point to Webflow site, collection, and page listing instructions. They do not inspect the local system or enumerate host files, users, processes, or environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed operations are Webflow API discovery steps needed for legitimate CMS workflows. No evidence of host reconnaissance appears in the reviewed file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Permanent CMS Deletion Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The guide includes DELETE_COLLECTION_ITEM for permanently removing CMS items. This is legitimate Webflow automation but should require explicit user confirmation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The deletion tool is named directly and the file states that deletion permanently removes CMS items. The risk is operational rather than malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: Whole-Site Publish Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The guide includes PUBLISH_SITE, which deploys all staged changes to selected domains. This can affect production content beyond the current item.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly states that publishing deploys all staged changes and is production-affecting. It also advises confirmation, so this is a managed operational risk.

content.value_statement: Webflow operations often require careful tool sequencing and exact IDs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for CMS, publishing, assets, pages, and orders.
actual_capabilities: Search current Rube MCP tool schemas before using Webflow tools.
Create, update, list, retrieve, and delete Webflow CMS collection items.
List Webflow sites, inspect site details, and publish staged changes.
List pages, retrieve page metadata, and inspect static page DOM structures.
Upload assets with file names, content types, base64 content, and MD5 checksums.
List and retrieve Webflow ecommerce orders when ecommerce is enabled.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Webflow OAuth connection.
Cannot bypass missing Webflow OAuth scopes or account permissions.
Publishing and deletion can affect production content and need confirmation.
Ecommerce order workflows are read-only in the documented tool set.

use_cases.title: Update CMS Content Safely
use_cases.description: Find the right collection fields, update CMS items, and publish only after confirming production impact.

use_cases.title: Inspect Site Structure
use_cases.description: List sites, pages, collections, and page DOM data before planning Webflow changes.

use_cases.title: Review Store Orders
use_cases.description: Retrieve Webflow ecommerce orders and inspect order details for reporting or support workflows.

prompt_templates.title: List My Webflow Sites
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Webflow automation skill to find my available Webflow sites. Search Rube tools first, then list sites and summarize the site names and IDs.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a CMS Item Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to update a Webflow CMS item. First discover the site, collection, and field schema. Ask me to confirm the item and fields before changing anything.

prompt_templates.title: Publish Confirmed Site Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Webflow publishing workflow for my selected site. Show the target domains and ask for explicit confirmation before publishing staged changes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit CMS and Asset Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to inspect collections, item fields, asset upload requirements, and publishing constraints for a Webflow content migration plan.

output_examples.input: List my Webflow sites and show which one has ecommerce enabled.
output_examples.output: A concise site inventory with site names, IDs, ecommerce availability, and next recommended tool calls.

output_examples.input: Create a draft blog post in the Blog collection.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step CMS workflow that identifies the collection, validates required fields, creates a draft item, and waits before publishing.

output_examples.input: Publish my latest CMS updates to the Webflow subdomain.
output_examples.output: A confirmation summary showing the site, selected publish target, known rate limit, and the production impact before execution.

best_practices: Search Rube tool schemas before each workflow because tool parameters can change.
Resolve site, collection, item, page, and domain IDs before making write operations.
Ask for explicit user confirmation before publishing or deleting CMS content.
anti_patterns: Do not use display field names when Webflow requires field slugs.
Do not publish immediately after a CMS edit without checking staged changes.
Do not upload placeholder text when the asset tool requires real base64 file content.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Webflow API key?
faq.answer: No. The documented setup uses Rube MCP and a Webflow OAuth connection through Composio.

faq.question: Can it publish a Webflow site?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents Webflow site publishing, but the user should confirm the target domains first.

faq.question: Can it delete CMS items?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes permanent CMS item deletion, so deletion should require explicit confirmation.

faq.question: Can it manage ecommerce orders?
faq.answer: It can list and retrieve orders when ecommerce is enabled. The documented order tools are read-only.

faq.question: Why does it search tools first?
faq.answer: Rube tool schemas can change. Searching first helps the assistant use current parameters.

faq.question: What permissions are needed?
faq.answer: Permissions depend on the workflow. The skill mentions scopes such as sites, CMS, and pages access.
